name: machine-touchpad
description: Capacitive touch sensor interface. ESP32 has 10 touch-sensitive GPIO pins. Can also wake from deep sleep.
differences_from_cpython: Does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific. Only available on original ESP32 (not S2/S3/C3).

classes.name: TouchPad

methods.name: TouchPad.read

returns.description: Touch reading. Lower value = more touch. Typical ranges: 100-1000 (no touch), 10-100 (touched).

methods.description: Read capacitive touch value. The exact threshold for 'touched' depends on the board/pin/wiring.

methods.name: TouchPad.config
methods.description: Set touch detection threshold. Used with esp32.wake_on_touch().

classes.example:
from machine import TouchPad, Pin
import time

touch = TouchPad(Pin(4))

# Calibrate: read baseline (no touch)
baseline = 0
for _ in range(100):
    baseline += touch.read()
    time.sleep_ms(10)
baseline //= 100
print(f'Baseline: {baseline}')

# Detect touch
while True:
    val = touch.read()
    if val < baseline * 0.7:  # 30% drop = touched
        print('Touched! Value:', val)
    time.sleep_ms(100)

esp32_notes: Only on original ESP32 (not ESP32-S2/S3/C3 — those use different touch API).
Touch pins can wake from deep sleep via esp32.wake_on_touch(True).
Touch readings are affected by: proximity to ground plane, trace length, humidity, PCB material.
For reliable touch, calibrate baseline at startup and use a percentage threshold.
Can be used as simple capacitive proximity sensor (not just touch buttons).
